name: Suspicious Amount of Files Renamed on the Same Machine (Windows)
notes: This rule triggers when a suspicious amount of files are renamed from Powershell. This could indicate the presence of a ransomware, which typically encrypt and rename files as part of their process.
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following Audit.Command Execution Success
when the event matches LOWER("Command") MATCHES '.*?rename-item.*' AQL filter query
when at least 25 events are seen with the same Machine Identifier (custom) in 5 minutes
thresholds: when at least 25 events are seen with the same Machine Identifier (custom) in 5 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Suspicious Amount of Files Renamed on the Same Machine (Windows) on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following Audit.Command Execution Success
AND when the event matches LOWER("Command") MATCHES '.*?rename-item.*' AQL filter query
AND when at least 25 events are seen with the same Machine Identifier (custom) in 5 minutes
RED_list: Event name: Suspicious Amount of Files Renamed on the Same Machine
Event description: Detects a suspicious amount of files renamed.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
